subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Water Tank — Rate Approximation, Left Riemann Sum, Quantity Estimate & IVT
stimulus:
Water is pumped into a tank at a rate modeled by $W(t) = 2000e^{-t^2/20}$ liters per hour for $0 \le t \le 8$, where $t$ is measured in hours. Water is removed from the tank at a rate modeled by $R(t)$ liters per hour, where $R$ is differentiable and decreasing on $0 \le t \le 8$. Selected values of $R(t)$ are shown in the table. At time $t = 0$, there are 50,000 liters of water in the tank.

| $t$ (hours) | 0 | 1 | 3 | 6 | 8 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| $R(t)$ (liters/hour) | 1340 | 1190 | 950 | 740 | 700 |

parts.prompt: Estimate $R'(2)$. Show the work that leads to your answer. Indicate units of measure.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $R'(2) \approx \frac{R(3) - R(1)}{3 - 1} = \frac{950 - 1190}{2} = -120$ liters/hr$^2$. Both estimate and units required.

alternatives.correct_example: $R'(2) \approx \frac{R(3) - R(1)}{3 - 1} = \frac{950 - 1190}{2} = -120$ liters per hour per hour

scoring_points.description: Units stated: liters per hour per hour (or liters per hour$^2$)

required_elements: Units stated: liters per hour per hour (or liters per hour$^2$)

parts.prompt: Use a left Riemann sum with the four subintervals indicated by the table to estimate the total amount of water removed from the tank during the 8 hours. Is this an overestimate or an underestimate of the total amount of water removed? Give a reason for your answer.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Left Riemann sum $= 1(1340) + 2(1190) + 3(950) + 2(740) = 8050$ liters. Overestimate since $R$ is decreasing.

scoring_points.description: Overestimate with reason: this is an overestimate because $R$ is a decreasing function (left endpoints give values larger than function values over each subinterval)

required_elements: Overestimate with reason: this is an overestimate because $R$ is a decreasing function (left endpoints give values larger than function values over each subinterval)

parts.prompt: Use your answer from part (b) to find an estimate of the total amount of water in the tank, to the nearest liter, at the end of 8 hours.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $50000 + \int_0^8 W(t)\, dt - 8050 \approx 50000 + 7836.195 - 8050 \approx 49786$ liters.

parts.prompt: For $0 \le t \le 8$, is there a time $t$ when the rate at which water is pumped into the tank is the same as the rate at which water is removed from the tank? Explain why or why not.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $W(0) - R(0) > 0$ and $W(8) - R(8) < 0$. Since $W(t) - R(t)$ is continuous, by the Intermediate Value Theorem there exists $t \in (0, 8)$ with $W(t) = R(t)$.

scoring_points.description: By IVT, there exists at least one $t \in (0, 8)$ such that $W(t) - R(t) = 0$, i.e., $W(t) = R(t)$

required_elements: By IVT, there exists at least one $t \in (0, 8)$ such that $W(t) - R(t) = 0$, i.e., $W(t) = R(t)$
